agent_raw_output:
<tool_call>
<function=Edit>
<parameter=file_path>
src/palindrome.js
</parameter>
<parameter=content>
/**
 * Return true if s reads the same forwards and backwards.
 *
 * Should be case-insensitive and ignore whitespace.
 */
function isPalindrome(s) {
  const cleaned = s.toLowerCase().replace(/\s/g, "");
  return cleaned === [...cleaned].reverse().join("");
}

module.exports = { isPalindrome };
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

/**
 * Remove duplicate values while preserving first-occurrence order.
 */
function uniq(items) {
  return [...new Set(items)];
}

module.exports = { uniq };
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

agent_raw_output:
All three tests now pass. Here's a summary of the bugs I fixed:

1. **`src/chunk.js`** - The `chunk` function was using `Math.floor(items.length / n)` which caused the last chunk to be truncated when there was a remainder. Changed to `Math.ceil(items.length / n)` to ensure all items are included in the chunks.

2. **`src/palindrome.js`** - The `isPalindrome` function was not handling case-insensitivity and whitespace. Added `.toLowerCase()` to convert to lowercase and `.replace(/\s/g, "")` to remove all whitespace before comparing.

3. **`src/uniq.js`** - The `uniq` function was sorting the result with `.sort()`, which broke the first-occurrence order requirement. Removed the `.sort()` call to preserve the original order.

shadow.reason: All 6 tests now pass, confirming all three bugs were fixed and verified.
shadow.summary: All three bugs are fixed and verified: chunk.js now uses Math.ceil to handle remainders, palindrome.js now lowercases and removes spaces, and uniq.js now preserves first-occurrence order without sorting. All tests in test/*.test.js pass. Task is complete.
